title: keel.toml policy document
description: The complete schema for keel.toml, expressed over its TOML-as-JSON form. This is contracts-v1: changes require an approved contract-change request (CCR). The same document, parsed to JSON, is what front ends pass to keel_configure(). Schedule strings follow contracts/schedule-grammar.ebnf. Target keys follow the target grammar in $defs.targetKey.

defaults.description: Baseline policy applied to any target that does not override a given layer. Resolution is per-layer replacement: a target that sets `retry` replaces the whole default `retry` table, never a deep merge.

llm.description: Defaults for all `llm:*` targets; resolved between target-specific config and defaults.outbound.

target.description: Per-target policy tables, keyed by the target grammar.

flows.description: Tier 2: durable flow designation. Config-only; never a code decorator (NFR1).

journal.description: Journal location. Default `file:.keel/journal.db`. Changing this string is the entire laptop-to-enterprise migration.

duration.description: Duration literal: integer + unit (ms, s, m, h).

rate.description: Rate literal: N per unit window. Units: s|sec, min, h|hour.

schedule.description: Retry schedule expression per contracts/schedule-grammar.ebnf, e.g. `exp(200ms, x2, max 30s, jitter)`, `fixed(1s)`, `exp(1s, x2, max 5m) upTo 10m andThen fixed(1m)`.

errorCondition.description: Structured error class a retry condition can match: transport classes, HTTP status classes (5xx/4xx), or an exact 3-digit status.

targetKey.description: Target grammar. One of: bare host or host/path glob (optionally METHOD-prefixed), `py:`/`ts:`/`rs:` function-path glob, `llm:<provider>`, `tool:<name>`, `mcp:<server>`.

on.description: Error conditions that are retryable. Non-idempotent requests are NEVER retried regardless of this list (DX Level 0 hard rule); they terminate with KEEL-E014.

breaker.description: Circuit breaker. Two modes: consecutive-failure count (`failures`) or windowed failure rate (`window` + `failure_rate`). Setting `failures` selects count mode. The breaker observes post-retry call outcomes (layer order: cache, rate, breaker, timeout, retry).

failures.description: Count mode: consecutive terminal failures before the breaker opens.

mode.description: `dev` caches only when KEEL_ENV != prod — the LLM dev-loop cache.

budget.description: Per-run spend/token budget for llm: targets, e.g. `$5/run`. Exceeding it trips the breaker.

fallback.description: Model fallback chain for llm: targets.
